Select Create image. If you are not sign: Select Create image. If you are not signed in, the account panel opens automatically. Create an account or sign in, then verify your email to keep creating images after your registration day.
Use a strong password or a passkey.
Keep your recovery codes somewhere safe: Keep your recovery codes somewhere safe when you enable 2FA.
Write a clear prompt with subject, setti: Write a clear prompt with subject, setting, composition, lighting, and style. You can add reference images when appearance or layout matters.
Use prompt tools to rewrite, analyze a r: Use prompt tools to rewrite, analyze a reference image, or translate when available.
Select aspect ratio and resolution, revi: Select aspect ratio and resolution, review your references, then start generation. Only one request can remain pending for your account at a time.
Square works well for avatars and produc: Square works well for avatars and product thumbnails.
Portrait ratios fit posters and social m: Portrait ratios fit posters and social media posts.
The Created images page shows queued, re: The Created images page shows queued, reviewing, generating, saving, and completed states. Open a completed image to download, edit, publish, or create a similar version.
Retry failed requests without rewriting: Retry failed requests without rewriting the prompt.
Publishing is optional; new images stay: Publishing is optional; new images stay private by default.

Created images lists every request owned: Created images lists every request owned by your account. Filter by status or publication state, sort recent work, and watch pending jobs update automatically.
Pending and successful generations count: Pending and successful generations count toward the daily web quota.
Administrators are not limited by daily: Administrators are not limited by daily web quota.
Edit or create a similar image: Edit or create a similar image
Edit image opens the original prompt and: Edit image opens the original prompt and image as references for a new generation. Create similar image reuses only the prompt. Your original result is never overwritten.
Use Edit image when the current composit: Use Edit image when the current composition should remain recognizable.
Use Create similar image when you only w: Use Create similar image when you only want the idea or writing style.
Publish makes a successful image visible: Publish makes a successful image visible in the public gallery. Unpublish removes it from discovery while keeping it in your account.
Review the prompt and reference images b: Review the prompt and reference images before publishing.
Only publish content you are allowed to: Only publish content you are allowed to share.
Browse categories and tags, search title: Browse categories and tags, search titles or prompts, save favorites, copy a public prompt, or start your own version from a published image.

Generate and protect your API key: Generate and protect your API key
Open Account, then API key. Each account: Open Account, then API key. Each account has one active key. Regenerating invalidates the previous token immediately while preserving usage statistics.
Send the token only in the Authorization: Send the token only in the Authorization header.
Never place an API key in browser code,: Never place an API key in browser code, screenshots, or a public repository.
Use JSON for prompt-only requests or mul: Use JSON for prompt-only requests or multipart form data when you include reference images. Requests are accepted only through the API subdomain. The prompt is required and accepts up to 1200 words.
POST /api/ai/images/publish creates and: POST /api/ai/images/publish creates and publishes the result.
API quota is separate from daily web quo: API quota is separate from daily web quota. One quota is charged only after a successful generation. Validation, moderation, quota, and provider failures do not consume it.
Use the latest request logs to diagnose: Use the latest request logs to diagnose status codes and duration.
Regenerating a token does not reset quot: Regenerating a token does not reset quota or logs.
Treat 401 as an invalid key, 403 as a bl: Treat 401 as an invalid key, 403 as a blocked account, 422 as invalid or rejected input, 429 as exhausted quota, and 503 as a temporary review failure.
Do not retry 401, 403, 422, or 429 witho: Do not retry 401, 403, 422, or 429 without correcting the cause.
Use bounded retries with backoff for tem: Use bounded retries with backoff for temporary 503 or network failures.
API key settings with masked key, quota,: API key settings with masked key, quota, statistics, and logs
API key settings: keep credentials hidde: API key settings: keep credentials hidden while monitoring usage.
Reveal or copy the key only when you are: Reveal or copy the key only when you are ready to store it securely.
Check used and remaining lifetime API qu: Check used and remaining lifetime API quota.
Review recent request status, duration,: Review recent request status, duration, and safe error summaries.
Quick answers about quota, privacy, acco: Quick answers about quota, privacy, account access, and common generation problems.

GenAnh gallery with sidebar navigation,: GenAnh gallery with sidebar navigation, sorting, and image creation button
Start from the gallery: create an image,: Start from the gallery: create an image, browse categories, or open the guide at any time.
Use the main navigation to open tools, s: Use the main navigation to open tools, search, favorites, and this guide.
Sort the gallery to discover new or feat: Sort the gallery to discover new or featured work.
Select Create image from any page when y: Select Create image from any page when you are signed in.
Are my generated images public by defaul: Are my generated images public by default?
No. A new image stays private in your ac: No. A new image stays private in your account. It appears in the community gallery only after you publish it.
What is the difference between web quota: What is the difference between web quota and API quota?
Web quota limits daily generations from: Web quota limits daily generations from the site. API quota is a separate lifetime allowance for authenticated API requests.
Why can I not create another image?: Why can I not create another image?
Check whether another request is still p: Check whether another request is still pending, your daily quota is exhausted, or your email needs verification. The Created images page shows pending work and remaining usage.
Does editing replace my original image?: Does editing replace my original image?
No. Edit image creates a new child resul: No. Edit image creates a new child result using the original prompt and image as references. The original remains unchanged.
When does an API request consume quota?: When does an API request consume quota?
API quota is charged only after generati: API quota is charged only after generation succeeds. Invalid input, rejected content, exhausted quota, and provider failures are not charged.
What should I do if an image fails?: What should I do if an image fails?
Open the failed image to read its safe e: Open the failed image to read its safe error message, then retry. Adjust the prompt or reference image if validation or content review rejected the request.
How do I secure my account?: How do I secure my account?
Verify your email, use a unique password: Verify your email, use a unique password or passkey, enable two-factor authentication, and store recovery codes offline.
What happens when I regenerate an API ke: What happens when I regenerate an API key?
The old token stops working immediately. Your quota, request statistics, and logs remain attached to the same key record.
Each guide focuses on one outcome, with: Each guide focuses on one outcome, with real screens and numbered notes showing exactly where to act.
Clear answers for the decisions that aff: Clear answers for the decisions that affect privacy, usage, and security.
Open the relevant image or request log f: Open the relevant image or request log first. Its current status and safe error message usually identify the next step.
Replace the placeholder token and local: Replace the placeholder token and local image path before running this request.
The example uses a placeholder. Keep the: The example uses a placeholder. Keep the real key in a server-side secret manager or environment variable, never in client-side code.
Put the guide into practice, or continue: Put the guide into practice, or continue with the next topic when you need a deeper workflow.

Mobile composer: the same workflow remai: Mobile composer: the same workflow remains available in a focused full-screen panel.
